description: Energy technology archetypes for pre-feasibility assessment. Ranges and defaults, not engineering precision.

technologies.name: Ground-Mounted Solar PV
technologies.description: Utility or community-scale ground-mounted photovoltaic systems
technologies.applicability: Best for areas with available open land, good solar resource, and grid access within 5km

technologies.name: Rooftop Solar PV
technologies.description: Distributed rooftop photovoltaic systems on commercial or residential buildings
technologies.applicability: Best for urban/suburban areas with existing building stock and net-metering policies

technologies.name: Battery Energy Storage (Li-ion)
technologies.description: Grid-scale or behind-the-meter lithium-ion battery storage
technologies.applicability: Pairs with solar PV for peak shaving, arbitrage, or grid stability. Useful where grid reliability is low.

technologies.name: Distribution Grid Upgrade
technologies.description: Upgrades to medium/low voltage distribution network to improve reliability and capacity
technologies.applicability: Needed when existing grid is at capacity or has high technical losses (>15%)

technologies.name: Building Energy Efficiency Bundle
technologies.description: Package of efficiency measures: LED lighting, HVAC upgrades, insulation, smart controls
technologies.applicability: Best for public buildings, schools, hospitals. Quick wins with short payback periods.

technologies.name: Distributed Micro-Generation
technologies.description: Community-scale distributed generation: micro-solar, small wind, micro-hydro
technologies.applicability: For underserved communities with limited grid access. Can improve energy equity and resilience.
